     The Numbers Business Report

Assault On Precinct 13 (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,040,895Details
International Box Office $16,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $36,040,895
Further financial details...

Synopsis

While en route to prison, a bus carrying criminals Marion, Beck and Anna is rerouted to Detroit’s soon-to-be-closed Precinct 13 because of a dangerous winter storm. All communications with the outside world are cut off, and Jake, an emotionally volatile police sergeant who is haunted by his past, is forced to work with the criminals in order to survive an attack on the precinct by a group of armed men.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,502,724 (32.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.08 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,297 opening theaters/2,297 max. theaters, 3.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,393,000

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 19th, 2005 (Wide) by Focus/Rogue Pictures
International Releases: April 1st, 2005 (Wide) (Australia)
April 9th, 2005 (Limited) (New Zealand)
February 29th, 2024 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: May 10th, 2005 by Universal Home Entertainment, released as Assault on Precinct 13
June 27th, 2005 by Entertainment in Video, released as Assault on Precinct 13
MPA Rating: R for strong violence and language throughout, and for some drug content.
Running Time: 109 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Remake
Plot point: Corrupt Cops, Crime Boss, Surprise Twist
Social setting: Siege
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Rogue Pictures, Why Not Productions, Liaison Films
Production Countries: United States, France
Languages: English

Synopsis

While en route to prison, a bus carrying criminals Marion, Beck and Anna is rerouted to Detroit’s soon-to-be-closed Precinct 13 because of a dangerous winter storm. All communications with the outside world are cut off, and Jake, an emotionally volatile police sergeant who is haunted by his past, is forced to work with the criminals in order to survive an attack on the precinct by a group of armed men.
